title: Camp Board
description: A physical project board for tracking shared builds, tasks, suggestions, locations, and materials on Fabric servers.
body:
# Camp Board

A physical project board for camps, towns, SMPs, and community builds.

Camp Board adds a placeable in-world board where players can organize shared projects, track tasks, list needed materials, suggest new builds, and keep completed work archived without relying on Discord pins, signs everywhere, or memory alone.

## What It Does

Camp Board is built for collaborative servers where players are constantly asking:

- What are we building next?
- What materials do we still need?
- Who is helping with this project?
- Where is this build located?
- What ideas have players suggested?

Place a Camp Board, open it, and use it as a shared planning hub.

## Features

### Physical Camp Board

- Placeable Camp Board block
- Freestanding placement
- Wall-mounted placement
- Custom model and texture
- Opens an in-game project board UI

### Project Tracking

- Create official server projects
- Track project descriptions
- Assign project leaders
- Set project status
- Archive completed or inactive projects

Project statuses include:

- Planned
- Active
- Paused
- Back Burner
- Completed
- Archived

### Tasks

Break projects into smaller tasks so players can see what needs doing.

Players can mark that they are helping with a task, and project leaders/admins can mark tasks as complete.

### Materials

Track requested materials for large builds.

Camp Board uses virtual material storage so players can contribute items directly through the board and withdraw them later if needed.

Useful for builds planned with tools like Litematica, where you know exactly how many blocks are needed.

### Suggestions

Players can submit project suggestions separately from official projects.

Admins can review suggestions and decide what should become an official project.

### Locations

Projects can store build coordinates.

Camp Board automatically pairs Overworld and Nether coordinates so players can plan routes from either dimension.

### Archive

Completed and inactive projects can be moved into the archive instead of being deleted.

Keep the board clean without losing the history of what your server has built.

## Server Friendly

Camp Board is designed for small, trust-based servers.

Data is saved as editable JSON inside the world folder:

`serverconfig/campboard/`

Per-board project data is stored in:

`serverconfig/campboard/boards/`

The mod also includes automatic backups and admin maintenance commands.

## Integrations

### JEI

Camp Board includes optional JEI support.

With JEI installed, Camp Board can appear in search with extra info and recipe support.

### Mod Menu

Camp Board includes optional Mod Menu support for basic configuration.

## Crafting Recipe

| Slot | Item |
|---|---|
| Top Left | Spruce Planks |
| Top Middle | Spruce Planks |
| Top Right | Spruce Planks |
| Middle Left | Spruce Planks |
| Center | Book |
| Middle Right | Spruce Planks |
| Bottom Left | Chest |
| Bottom Middle | Birch Sign |
| Bottom Right | Chest |

Recipe layout:

|  |  |  |
|---|---|---|
| Spruce Planks | Spruce Planks | Spruce Planks |
| Spruce Planks | Book | Spruce Planks |
| Chest | Birch Sign | Chest |

## Requirements

- Minecraft Java 26.1.2
- Fabric Loader 0.18.4 or newer
- Fabric API 0.150.0+26.1.2 or newer
- Java 25

## Install Info

Camp Board must be installed on both the server and every client joining the server.

- Environment: Client and Server
- Requirement: Required on both

## Good For

- SMP servers
- Town projects
- Community builds
- Build planning
- Material collection
- Shared task boards
- Camp/village coordination

## Notes

This is an early beta release intended for testing before use on a live server.

Back up your world before installing any new mod on a server.
